children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2), the defense commissary system and the exchange stores system shall be operated as separate systems of the Department of Defense.

children.label: A
children.text: Combined exchange and commissary stores operated under the authority provided by .section 2489 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: NEXMART stores of the Navy Exchange Service Command established before .October 1, 2003

children.heading: .—Consolidation or Other Organizational Changes of Defense Retail Systems
children.label: b .—Consolidation or Other Organizational Changes of Defense Retail Systems

children.text: The operation and administration of the defense retail systems may not be consolidated or otherwise merged unless the consolidation or merger is specifically authorized by an Act of Congress.

In this subsection, the term “defense retail systems” means the defense commissary system and exchange stores system and other revenue-generating facilities operated by nonappropriated fund instrumentalities of the Department of Defense for the morale, welfare, and recreation of members of the armed forces.

children.label: A
children.text: to exploit synergies between the defense commissary system and the exchange system; and

children.label: B
children.text: to optimize the operations of the defense retail systems as a whole and the benefits provided by the commissaries and exchanges.

children.label: A
children.text: for products and services that are shared by the defense commissary system and the exchange system; and

children.label: B
children.text: for the acquisition of supplies, resale goods, and services on behalf of both the defense commissary system and the exchange system.

children.label: A
children.text: use funds appropriated pursuant to to reimburse a nonappropriated fund entity or instrumentality for the portion of the cost of a contract or agreement entered by the nonappropriated fund entity or instrumentality that is attributable to the defense commissary system; andsection 2483 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: authorize the defense commissary system to accept reimbursement from a nonappropriated fund entity or instrumentality for the portion of the cost of a contract or agreement entered by the defense commissary system that is attributable to the nonappropriated fund entity or instrumentality.

children.heading: .—Access of Exchange Stores System to Federal Financing Bank
children.label: d .—Access of Exchange Stores System to Federal Financing Bank
children.text: To facilitate the provision of in-store credit to patrons of the exchange stores system while reducing the costs of providing such credit, the Army and Air Force Exchange Service, Navy Exchange Service Command, and Marine Corps exchanges may issue and sell their obligations to the Federal Financing Bank as provided in section 6 of the Federal Financing Bank Act of 1973 ().12 U.S.C. 2285
